title: Mega Kangaskhan Battle Guide
summary: A Mega physical ace whose [parental-bond] converts every attack into a double-strike — [fake-out] with [parental-bond] is one of the most broken tempo plays in the format.
strengths: [parental-bond] converts every move into a double-strike
[fake-out] doubles into ~200 BP plus flinch pressure
weaknesses: Fighting 2x weakness is unchanged after Mega

recommendedMoves.reason: Ground coverage for Steel and Electric walls
Priority cleanup doubled to two hits
Tempo control plus guaranteed flinch doubled

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternatives

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Kangaskhan is a top-tier physical ace defined entirely by [parental-bond] — the ability makes every attack hit twice (primary hit at full damage, secondary at 25%). This doubles [fake-out] into effectively 80 BP plus two flinch chances, [double-edge] into ~150 BP, and [sucker-punch] into two priority hits. 105/100/100 post-Mega bulk also stabilises the set for repeated entries.

## Strengths
[parental-bond] is the strongest single-pokemon ability in the metagame offensive pool. Every attack is functionally a double-strike, which turns [fake-out] into a near-guaranteed two-turn tempo lock and [double-edge] into one of the heaviest hitters in the format. [sucker-punch] priority doubles into two hits, pushing Mega Kangaskhan safely above most scarfed threats. focus-sash users are also bypassed on the first attack because the second hit triggers instantly.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Fighting 2x is still live, and close-combat / dynamic-punch users remain hard counters. Speed 100 is outrun by 110+ upper-tier attackers, which means a scarfed Fighting type wins the race cleanly. The pre-Mega turn runs scrappy, so [parental-bond] does not activate until the evolution completes — the first turn needs to be a safe entry.

## Recommended Moveset
[double-edge] is the locked [normal] STAB because the doubled hit is the whole damage plan. [earthquake] is the Ground coverage for Steel, Rock, and Electric walls. [sucker-punch] is the priority cleanup move doubled into two hits for guaranteed KOs on weakened fast threats. [fake-out] is the lead slot for tempo control — the doubled flinch is effectively a free turn.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[kangaskhanite] is mandatory. [parental-bond] activates post-evolution. Tera [normal] maxes [double-edge] damage, Tera [ghost] flips [normal] and Fighting weaknesses but interacts with scrappy differently — pick the Tera type based on team needs. Tera [ghost] defensive pivoting is strong but costs the [normal] STAB boost on [double-edge].

## Teammates & Synergy
Mega Kangaskhan is a [fake-out] lead that sets up partners. Pair it with setup sweepers that benefit from a free turn. Psychic or Fairy partners cover the Fighting weakness, and a hazard remover keeps the recoil plan sustainable. A cleaner behind Mega Kangaskhan finishes weakened cores after the tempo advantage sets in.

## Play Patterns
Lead with Mega Kangaskhan, evolve turn one, [fake-out] to steal tempo and flinch, then [double-edge] or [earthquake] on the weakened target. Use [sucker-punch] on faster priority-vulnerable threats, and Tera on a telegraphed Fighting move.
